content: I just finished reading 'The Art of Urban Sketching' and it's completely changed how I see my city. I've been carrying my sketchbook everywhere now.
That sounds like a fascinating book! What made you pick it up in the first place?
I've been getting into urban sketching for a few months now. I started after finding some old watercolor supplies in my parents' attic while helping them move to a smaller place in Denver.
That's such a nice connection to your parents. How has the sketching been going? Have you found good spots around Denver?
Yeah, I've been sketching at Union Station and the Denver Art Museum. I actually post my sketches on my travel blog - I'm trying to combine my love of art with my travel writing.
That's a great combination! Do you focus on Denver scenes or do you sketch when you travel too?

Both, actually. Last month I was in Austin for a real estate conference and I spent an extra day sketching the murals around South Congress. Being a real estate agent gives me good travel opportunities.
The book also has some great tips about materials. I've been experimenting with different papers - I found this amazing handmade paper at a shop in Boulder that's perfect for urban scenes.
That sounds wonderful! Handmade paper can make such a difference. Do you have a favorite sketching spot in Denver that you keep going back to?
Definitely Confluence Park. There's something about the mix of the river, the city skyline, and all the people that makes it perfect for sketching. I try to go there every Saturday morning.
